is [clothing](/wiki/Clothing "Clothing") worn for very special occasions, such as coronations, graduations, parades, religious rites, trials and other important events. In the [western dress code](/wiki/Western_dress_codes "Western dress codes") [hierarchy](/wiki/Hierarchy "Hierarchy") of [dress codes](/wiki/Dress_code "Dress code"), ceremonial dress is often considered one of the most [formal](/wiki/Formal_attire "Formal attire"), in other cultures ceremonial dresses vary widely having entirely different meanings, and styles.

Driscoll-Engelstad, Bernadette (2005). ["Dance of the Loon: Symbolism and Continuity in Copper Inuit Ceremonial Clothing"](https://www.jstor.org/stable/pdf/40316636.pdf) (PDF). *Arctic Anthropology*. **42** (1): 33–46. [doi](/wiki/Doi_(identifier) "Doi (identifier)"):[10.1353/arc.2011.0010](https://doi.org/10.1353%2Farc.2011.0010). [JSTOR](/wiki/JSTOR_(identifier) "JSTOR (identifier)") [40316636](https://www.jstor.org/stable/40316636). From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Clothing primarily reserved for ritual or ceremonial purposes

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/44/Edward_VII_in_coronation_robes.jpg/250px-Edward_VII_in_coronation_robes.jpg)](/wiki/File:Edward_VII_in_coronation_robes.jpg)

State portrait of King [Edward VII](/wiki/Edward_VII "Edward VII") of the United Kingdom, showing the coronation regalia atop a [full dress](/wiki/Full_dress "Full dress") military uniform

| [Semi-formal](/wiki/Semi-formal_wear "Semi-formal wear") ([half dress](/wiki/Western_dress_codes#Full_dress,_half_dress,_and_undress "Western dress codes"))  * [Black tie](/wiki/Black_tie "Black tie") * [Black lounge suit](/wiki/Black_lounge_suit "Black lounge suit") * [Mess dress uniform](/wiki/Mess_dress_uniform "Mess dress uniform") * [Evening gown](/wiki/Evening_gown "Evening gown")    + [Cocktail dress](/wiki/Cocktail_dress "Cocktail dress") |

| [Semi-informal](/w/index.php?title=Semi-informal_wear&action=edit&redlink=1 "Semi-informal wear (page does not exist)") ([Uniform](/wiki/Uniform "Uniform"), [undress](/wiki/Western_dress_codes#Full_dress,_half_dress,_and_undress "Western dress codes"), "professional casual")  * [Combat uniform](/wiki/Combat_uniform "Combat uniform") * [Smart casual](/wiki/Smart_casual "Smart casual") * [Business casual](/wiki/Business_casual "Business casual") * [Workwear](/wiki/Workwear "Workwear") * [School uniform](/wiki/School_uniform "School uniform") ([academic dress](/wiki/Academic_dress "Academic dress")) |
| [Casual](/wiki/Casual_wear "Casual wear") (anything not above)  * [Casual Friday](/wiki/Casual_Friday "Casual Friday") * [Streetwear](/wiki/Streetwear "Streetwear") * [Sportswear](/wiki/Sportswear "Sportswear") * [Swimwear](/wiki/Swimwear "Swimwear") * [Nightwear](/wiki/Nightwear "Nightwear") |
| Supplementary alternatives  * Ceremonial dress   + [law courts](/wiki/Court_dress "Court dress")   + [royal courts](/wiki/Court_uniform_and_dress_in_the_United_Kingdom "Court uniform and dress in the United Kingdom")   + [diplomatic](/wiki/Diplomatic_uniform "Diplomatic uniform") * [Religious clothing](/wiki/Religious_clothing "Religious clothing")   + [cassock](/wiki/Cassock "Cassock"), [habit](/wiki/Religious_habit "Religious habit"), etc. * [Folk costume](/wiki/Folk_costume "Folk costume") * Distinctions   + [Orders](/wiki/Order_(distinction) "Order (distinction)")   + [medals](/wiki/Medal "Medal"), etc. |
| Legend: = Day (before 6 p.m.)  = Evening (after 6 p.m.)     = [Bow tie](/wiki/Bow_tie "Bow tie") colour  = Ladies  = Gentlemen   ---   [Fashion portal](/wiki/Portal:Fashion "Portal:Fashion") |
| * [v](/wiki/Template:Western_dress_codes "Template:Western dress codes") * [t](/wiki/Template_talk:Western_dress_codes "Template talk:Western dress codes") * [e](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:Western_dress_codes "Special:EditPage/Template:Western dress codes") |

**Ceremonial dress** is [clothing](/wiki/Clothing "Clothing") worn for very special occasions, such as coronations, graduations, parades, religious rites, trials and other important events. In the [western dress code](/wiki/Western_dress_codes "Western dress codes") [hierarchy](/wiki/Hierarchy "Hierarchy") of [dress codes](/wiki/Dress_code "Dress code"), ceremonial dress is often considered one of the most [formal](/wiki/Formal_attire "Formal attire"), in other cultures ceremonial dresses vary widely having entirely different meanings, and styles.

## Examples

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ceremonial_dress&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Examples")]

There has been documented knowledge on the effects of ceremonial clothing, with those wearing ceremonial clothing have been used to denote a wide range of usage among varying unique [cultures](/wiki/Cultures "Cultures").[[1]](#cite_note-1) Examples of ceremonial dress include:

* royal [cloak](/wiki/Cloak "Cloak") ([ermine](/wiki/Stoat "Stoat") lined), [crown](/wiki/Crown_(headgear) "Crown (headgear)") and [scepter](/wiki/Scepter "Scepter") of a [monarch](/wiki/Monarch "Monarch")
* [court dress](/wiki/Court_dress "Court dress"), such as the robe and [wig](/wiki/Wig "Wig") commonly used in [Commonwealth countries](/wiki/Commonwealth_countries "Commonwealth countries")
* [diplomatic uniform](/wiki/Diplomatic_uniform "Diplomatic uniform")
* the [full dress uniforms](/wiki/Full_dress_uniform "Full dress uniform") of [military](/wiki/Military "Military") personnel (or ceremonial [suit of armor](/wiki/Suit_of_armor "Suit of armor"))
* [religious clothing](/wiki/Religious_clothing "Religious clothing"), such as liturgical [vestments](/wiki/Vestment "Vestment")
* [folk or tribal costumes](/wiki/Folk_costume "Folk costume") reserved for the most formal occasions
* [academic dress](/wiki/Academic_dress "Academic dress")
* [wedding clothing](/w/index.php?title=Wedding_clothing&action=edit&redlink=1 "Wedding clothing (page does not exist)"), including [wedding dresses](/wiki/Wedding_dress "Wedding dress")
